info.title: Call Verification
info.description:
The Bandwidth Call Verification API can be used to retrieve fraud and spoofing risk scores for specific phone calls that are
currently active on the Bandwidth network.

get.summary: Retrieve the risk scores for a specific phone call using to and from numbers and start time of call
get.description:
Use the to and from number and approximate start time of the call to retrieve fraud and spoofing risk scores for a specific phone call that is currently active on the Bandwidth network.

The fraud and spoofing scores returned by this API endpoint indicate how likely it is that the given phone call is
fraudulent or that the given phone call is coming from a spoofed phone number or not.

Currently this API endpoint only supports inbound phone calls to phone numbers that have the call verification feature
enabled, but it may support other types of calls in the future.

get.summary: Retrieve the risk scores for a specific phone call
get.description:
Retrieve fraud and spoofing risk scores for a specific phone call that is currently active on the Bandwidth network.

The fraud and spoofing scores returned by this API endpoint indicate how likely it is that the given phone call is 
fraudulent or that the given phone call is coming from a spoofed phone number.

Currently this API endpoint only supports inbound phone calls to phone numbers that have the call verification feature
enabled, but it may support other types of calls in the future.

**Note**: This information is generated after a call is answered and is cached for only 30 minutes.  If you attempt
to query this information before the score has been generated or after the 30 minute cache period expires, the API
may return an HTTP 404.

accountId.description: The ID of the Bandwidth App account that the called number of the phone call is assigned to.

activeCallId.description:
The unique Bandwidth-generated identifier of a phone call provided by Bandwidth in the SIP invite for that call.
Typically this value is provided in an X header in the SIP invite such as X-Customer-Ixn.

fromNumber.description: From phone number, in E.164 format, starting with '+' (plus) symbol.

toNumber.description: To phone number, in E.164 format, starting with '+' (plus) symbol.

startTime.description: Start time of the phone call.This field needs to be formatted in ISO-8601 format.

clockDrift.description:
Allows for some variance in the clock synchronization.
The value is measured in seconds. The value cannot be larger than 60 seconds. Minimum value is 0.
The value is split to equal time before and after. For example, a value of 20 will use drift variance of 10 seconds before to 10 seconds after.

activeCallInfo.description: All available information about a specific phone call that is currently active on the bandwidth network.

rel.description: Specifies the relationship between this link and the resource.

activeCallInfoWithoutCallId.description: All available information about a specific phone call that is currently active on the bandwidth network.

rel.description: Specifies the relationship between this link and the resource.

spoofingRisk.description:
A number between 0 and 100 that indicates the likelihood that the calling number on active phone call is 
being spoofed (with 0 being the lowest possible risk and 100 being the highest possible risk).

fraudRisk.description:
A number between 0 and 100 that indicates the level of risk that a phone call is spam or is otherwise coming
from a fraudulent source.

activeCallId.description:
The unique Bandwidth-generated identifier of a phone call provided by Bandwidth in the SIP invite for that call.
Typically this value is provided in an X header in the SIP invite such as X-Customer-Ixn.

riskScore.description: The risk score for a specific phone call.

spoofingRisk.description:
A number between 0 and 100 that indicates the likelihood that the calling number on active phone call is 
being spoofed (with 0 being the lowest possible risk and 100 being the highest possible risk).

fraudRisk.description:
A number between 0 and 100 that indicates the level of risk that a phone call is spam or is otherwise coming
from a fraudulent source.

Basic.description:
Basic authentication is a simple authentication scheme built into the HTTP protocol. To use it, send your HTTP requests with an `Authorization` header that contains the word `Basic` followed by a space and a Base64-encoded string `username:password`.

- Example: `Authorization: Basic ZGVtbZpwQDU1dzByZA==`

Bearer.description:
Bearer authentication (also called token authentication) is an HTTP authentication scheme that involves security tokens called bearer tokens. The name “Bearer authentication” can be understood as “give access to the bearer of this token.” The client must send this token in the `Authorization` header when making requests to protected resources.

- Example: `Authorization: Bearer <token>`

Where `<token>` should be replaced with the token string without angle brackets.

For more information, see the following guide: [Credentials](https://dev.bandwidth.com/docs/account/credentials/management)
